steps.title: Identify the genetic redistribution
steps.content: The key clue is the gene expression pattern: anterior genes active throughout the entire body, posterior genes limited to the edges.

steps.title: Interpret the genetic shift
steps.content:
The anterior region expands throughout the body while posterior shrank to the margins—sea stars evolved to expand the head and eliminate the trunk, inverting the traditional head-loss assumption.

The other choices misread the genetic distribution, deny the bilateral ancestor relationship, or falsely claim genetic correspondence doesn't exist.

That matches Choice D — rather than undergoing changes resulting in the eventual elimination of a head region in their radial body plan, as previously assumed, sea stars' morphology evolved to completely lack a trunk and consist primarily of a head region..
